div1.entryFree:
Glean, 1) to gather: to g. the broken ears, As III, 5, 102. --ing all the land's wealth into one, H8 III, 2, 284. when he needs what you have --ed, it is but squeezing, Hml. IV, 2, 21.
2) to pick out, to separate, to sift: how much low peasantry would then be --ed from the true seed of honour, Merch. II, 9, 46.
3) to obtain, to acquire: not for Bohemia, nor the pomp that may be thereat --ed, Wint. IV, 4, 500. a wonder how his grace should g. it, H5 I, 1, 53.
4) to disfurnish, to lay bare, to exhaust: galling the --ed land with hot assays, H5 I, 2, 151.
5) to conclude, to infer: what harm can your bisson conspectuities g. out of this character, Cor. II, 1, 71. and to gather, so much as from occasion you may g. Hml. II, 2, 16.
